message.content: {"items":[{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2010","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11606","justification":"The 5th EWCS (2010) includes extensive indicators on occupational health, safety, exposure to risks, psychosocial factors, and well-being at work. It covers EU member states and several associated countries, including many of the listed ones (e.g., Belgium, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Spain, Finland, France, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Austria, Portugal, Sweden, United Kingdom, Bulgaria, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Croatia, Norway). It is within the 2010s and hosted by GESIS."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2015","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12660","justification":"The 6th EWCS (2015) provides harmonized data on occupational health and safety, physical and psychosocial risk factors, work-related health outcomes, and working conditions. It covers a broad set of European countries, including most of the requested ones (EU member states plus some non-EU such as Norway). Fieldwork is in the mid‑2010s, aligning with the requested decade, and the dataset is hosted at GESIS."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2017 (Overview and Methodology)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA7633","justification":"The EWCS 2017 wave (see GESIS study ZA7633) continues the series on working conditions and occupational health across European countries. It contains variables on work-related health problems, exposure to hazards, and safety practices. Coverage includes many EU and associated countries in the late 2010s, matching the topic and period. (The DOI is linked from the GESIS landing page; the landing page itself is GESIS-hosted.)"},{"title":"European Company Survey 2013","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5963","justification":"The European Company Survey 2013 (ZA5963) focuses on establishments and includes information on workplace health and safety policies, health promotion measures, and working conditions at the firm level across many European countries (including most EU member states and some others). It falls squarely in the 2010s and is relevant to occupational health from an employer perspective. GESIS hosts the study landing page and access details."},{"title":"European Company Survey 2019","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA7649","justification":"The 2019 wave of the European Company Survey (ZA7649) covers workplace practices, occupational safety and health management, employee representation, and well-being in establishments across EU member states and other European countries. It provides late‑2010s data relevant to occupational health in many of the listed countries. Dataset documentation and access are via GESIS."},{"title":"European Survey of Enterprises on New and Emerging Risks (ESENER-2, 2014)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5981","justification":"ESENER-2 (ZA5981) by EU-OSHA focuses specifically on occupational safety and health, including management of psychosocial and physical risks at the enterprise level. The 2014 wave covers establishments in many European countries, including most of the EU member states you listed, and directly addresses occupational health in the 2010s. GESIS provides the study landing page and access."},{"title":"European Survey of Enterprises on New and Emerging Risks (ESENER-3, 2019)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA7640","justification":"ESENER-3 (ZA7640, 2019) continues the focus on occupational safety and health risk management, including psychosocial and organizational factors. It covers enterprises in EU countries and several other European states, addressing occupational health topics (risk assessment, worker participation, preventive measures) in the late 2010s. The data are hosted and documented by GESIS."},{"title":"5th European Working Conditions Survey, 2010 – GESIS Study Page","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5055","justification":"The GESIS study ZA5055 is the landing page for the 5th EWCS 2010, giving variables, methodology, and access links. It is particularly relevant for analyzing occupational health (self-reported health problems, exposure to physical and psychosocial risks) in the early 2010s across a large set of European countries that includes many you specified."},{"title":"6th European Working Conditions Survey, 2015 – GESIS Study Page","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA6584","justification":"The GESIS study ZA6584 documents the 6th EWCS 2015. It contains a wide range of occupational health and working-conditions indicators and covers the EU-28 plus some additional European countries. This makes it suitable for comparative occupational health research in the mid‑2010s for many of the listed countries."},{"title":"European Quality of Life Survey 2012 – GESIS Study Page","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5685","justification":"While broader than strictly occupational health, EQLS 2012 (ZA5685) contains important indicators on health, work–life balance, job quality, and working conditions at the individual level across European countries. It covers most EU member states and some neighboring countries in the early 2010s and can be used to study links between employment conditions and health outcomes, complementing more focused occupational health surveys."}]}
